content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission
January 25,
1993
Claudia (o1eman
Rees
& Associates Inc.
1121
T, Street,
Suite 808
Sacramento,
CA
95814
Re:
Your Request for Informa-l Assistance
Our File No.
1-93—18
Dear Ms.
Coleman:
-I
You have requested confirmation of advice provided to you at
an
FPPC workshop concerning the lobbying provisions of the
Political Reform Act (“Act”.)
1
!
Because you have not identified
the person on whose behalf you are seeking advice,
we treat your
letter as a request for informal assistance.
2
?
OUESTION
Does a lobbyist need to attend a lobbyist ethics course
during the 1993-94 Legislative Session if the lobbyist attended
the course on December 13,
1990 and executed his/her Lobbyist
Certification Form
(Form 604)
for the
1993—94 Legislative Session
oir December 1,
1992?
CONCLUSION
The lobbyist would not be requireii o take the ethics course
during the 1993-94 Legislative Session.
1/
Government Code Sections 81000-91015.
All statutory
references are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated.
Commission regulations appear at
2 California Code of Regulations
Section 18000,
et g.
All references to regulations are to Title
2,
Division
6 of the California Code of Regulations.
2/
Informal assistance does not provide the requestor with the
immunity provided by an opinion or formal written advice.
(Government Code Section 83114;
2 Cal.
Code of Regs.
Section
18329(c) (3) .)
428 J Street. Suite 8(X)
•
P.O. Box 807
•
Sicramentu, CA
95812
•
(916) 22-5660


1—93—18
Page
#
ANALYSTS
In 1990 legislation was enacted to require lobbyists to
attend an orientation course on the relevant ethical issues and
laws relating to lobbying.
(Chapter 84, Stats.
1990.)
Upon
filing a lobbyist certification a lobbyist must indicate whether
he/she has taken the course within the previous 24 months.
(Section 86103(d)(1).)
If more than 24 months has elapsed, the
lobbyist must attend a course within a reasonable period of time
and his/her certification is accepted on a conditional basis until
the course is taken.
(Section 86103(d) (2).)
According to your
facts,
when the lobbyist signed the verification section of the
Form 64
on December 1,
1992, he/she had taken the course within
the previous 24 months.
Therefore, the lobbyist is not required
to take the course for the 1993-94 Legislation Session
3
/
Please do not hesitate to contact meat
(916)
322—5662 if you
have additional questions.
Sincer ly,
tda Cassady
Political Reform Consultant
3/
Legislation may be proposed in the 1993-94 Legislative
Session to clarify this requirement for future registrations.
